ocr.text: The first table presents data on the SDQ metrics related to DBD, comprising Emotional Symptoms, Hyperactivity-Inattention,
Peer Problems, Conduct Problems, Prosocial Behavior, and Total Difficulties across different behavioral categories. The
Emotional Symptoms scores are .33 for Inattention, .32 for Hyperactivity/Impulsivity, .33 for ODD1, and .12 for CD2. The
Hyperactivity-Inattention scores are .64 for Inattention, .62 for Hyperactivity/Impulsivity, .45 for ODD1, and .28 for CD2. For
Peer Problems, the scores are .36 for Inattention, .33 for Hyperactivity/Impulsivity, .34 for ODD1, and .17 for CD2. In terms of
Conduct Problems, the values are .47 for Inattention, .58 for Hyperactivity/Impulsivity, .66 for ODD1, and .40 for CD2.
Physiographic Province  n  n >5 μg/L  % n > 5 μg/L
The Prosocial Behavior scores show -.36 for Inattention, -.36 for Hyperactivity/Impulsivity, -.38 for ODD1, and -.24 for CD2.
Lastly, the Total Difficulties scores are .63 for Inattention, .63 for Hyperactivity/Impulsivity, .59 for ODD1, and .32 for CD2.
The second table details the Physiographic Province, with n, n >5 μg/L, and % n > 5 μg/L values. For the Coastal Plain, n is
1162, n >5 μg/L is 29, and % n > 5 μg/L is 2.5. The Piedmont has n at 2211, n >5 μg/L at 47, and % n > 5 μg/L at 2.1. The Blue
Ridge reports n as 749, n >5 μg/L as 2, and % n > 5 μg/L as 0.3. The Valley and Ridge shows n at 1496, n >5 μg/L at 15, and %
n > 5 μg/L at 1.0. The Appalachian Plateau has n at 14, n >5 μg/L at 1, and % n > 5 μg/L at 7.1. The Total values for n is 5632, n
>5 μg/L is 94, and % n > 5 μg/L is 1.7.
